mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) across 100+ easing observations, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment remains below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which drives corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day returns in over 60% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.05 compared to single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints that compress profit margins and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat during industrial production contractions within tightening regimes, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy (AUC < 0.50) in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and forces market repricing, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are positive in >50% of high-inflation months, or Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the consumer base and supports aggregate demand, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60 trading day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with payroll growth (Spearman rho < 0.05) across 200+ monthly observations, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining real GDP growth and rising retail sales indicates unsustainable consumption debt accumulation, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as markets price in a demand collapse.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive 5-day returns in over 55% of cases, or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for monetary policy stance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase signals expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are negative in >60% of recovery-phase industrial expansions, or the signal shows no statistical edge (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.
